multiple_choice.text: What is the square root of 144?
What is 7 × 8?
Which is the largest prime number below 20?
What is 2 to the power of 10?
What is 15% of 200?
What comes next in the sequence: 2, 4, 8, 16, ___?
How many sides does a hexagon have?
What is 25% of 80?
What is 144 ÷ 12?
What is the value of π (pi) rounded to 2 decimal places?
What is 9² (nine squared)?
How many degrees are in a full circle?
What is 1/4 expressed as a percentage?
What is the LCM of 4 and 6?
A rectangle has length 8 and width 5. What is its area?
What is 0.75 expressed as a fraction?

fill_blank.text: A right angle measures ___ degrees.
The sum of all angles in a triangle equals ___ degrees.
A polygon with eight sides is called an ___.
A number divisible only by 1 and itself is called a ___ number.
The perimeter of a square with side length 7 is ___.
10 squared (10²) equals ___.
The square root of 81 is ___.
A triangle with all three sides equal is called ___.
50% of 60 equals ___.
In a right triangle, the longest side is called the ___.
The formula for the area of a rectangle is length × ___.
The number that has no value is ___.
The result of multiplying a number by itself is called a perfect ___.
3/6 simplified to its lowest form is ___.
The perimeter of a circle is called its ___.
A number that can be divided into two equal whole numbers is called ___.

open_question.text: Explain the difference between a prime number and a composite number. Give one example of each.
State the Pythagorean theorem and write its formula. When is it used?
What is the order of operations in mathematics? Explain using BODMAS and give an example.
How do you find the area and perimeter of a rectangle? Give the formulas and a worked example.
What is a fraction? Explain the difference between a proper fraction, an improper fraction, and a mixed number.
Describe what a variable is in algebra. Write and explain a simple equation that uses a variable.
What is the difference between mean, median, and mode? Give a set of numbers and calculate all three.
Explain what negative numbers are. Give two real-world examples where negative numbers are used.
What is a ratio? Explain how to simplify a ratio and give an example.
Describe the difference between perimeter and area. Why are their units different?
What is a percentage? How do you convert a fraction to a percentage? Give two examples.
Explain what probability means. If you flip a fair coin, what is the probability of getting heads? Why?

multiple_choice.text: What is the capital city of Australia?
Which is the largest ocean on Earth?
What is the smallest country in the world?
Which is the tallest mountain on Earth?
How many continents are there on Earth?
What is the capital city of Japan?
Which country has the most natural lakes in the world?
Which is the largest hot desert in the world?
What is the capital city of Canada?
On which continent is the country of Brazil located?
What is the longest river in South America?
Which country is both a continent and a country?
What is the capital of Egypt?
Which ocean lies between Africa and Australia?
Mount Kilimanjaro is located in which country?
Which is the most populous country in the world?

fill_blank.text: The longest river in the world is the ___.
Russia is the largest country in the world by ___.
The Amazon Rainforest is located in ___ America.
The capital city of France is ___.
Mount Everest is located in the ___ mountain range.
The Sahara Desert is in ___ Africa.
The currency used in Japan is the ___.
The world's largest coral reef system is the Great ___ Reef.
The ocean between Europe and North America is the ___ Ocean.
The capital city of Brazil is ___.
The ___ is the imaginary line that divides Earth into northern and southern hemispheres.
The driest continent on Earth is ___.
The Eiffel Tower is located in the city of ___.
New Zealand's capital city is ___.
The tallest waterfall in the world is ___ Falls.
The capital city of South Africa's legislative branch is ___.

open_question.text: What is the difference between a country, a nation, and a state? Give one example of each.
Explain what causes the four seasons on Earth. Why do the northern and southern hemispheres have opposite seasons?
What is a time zone and why do we need them? Approximately how many time zones does the world have?
Describe the water cycle. Name and explain its four main stages.
What is climate change? Name two human activities that contribute to it and one consequence.
Explain the difference between weather and climate. Give one example of each.
What is a peninsula? Name two famous peninsulas in the world and which countries they belong to.
What are tectonic plates? Explain how their movement can cause earthquakes and volcanic eruptions.
What is deforestation? Describe two causes and two environmental effects of deforestation.
Explain what latitude and longitude are. How are they used to locate places on Earth?
What is the difference between a river delta and a river estuary? Give one example of each.
What are natural resources? Give three examples and explain why their conservation is important.
